summary: A mixed‑quality matrix recording captures a jam‑heavy fall‑'76 show with standout performances of Crazy Fingers, Scarlet Begonias, the St. Stephen > The Other One medley and an inspired Music Never Stopped/Mind Left Body jam.
review: Fans rave about the deep, improvisational vibe of this late‑1976 set – highlighting a marathon Crazy Fingers, a hopping Scarlet Begonias, a fiery St. Stephen > The Other One/Wharf Rat medley and the rare dual Music Never Stopped/Mind Left Body jam.  The source is a patchwork of audience tape with some sound‑board splices, giving it a marginal but listenable clarity.  Overall the musical payoff outweighs the uneven recording fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show positively (4‑5 stars) and praise several jams, while recording quality is described as marginal or mixed; weighting performance higher yields a solid mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers note decent sound on the AUD portion, but others describe distance, muffled hand‑held capture and marginal overall fidelity; a few minutes of soundboard are spliced in.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The intimate Mershon venue encouraged a laid‑back, jam‑focused atmosphere; fans note the gradual tightening of the band in late ’76 with seamless transitions and extended improvisations.
